zh.lifeSummary: De Feni於1060年出生。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: De Feni于1060年出生。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Feni
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name in south-east England for someone who lived in a low-lying marshy area, from Middle English fen(ne) ‘fen, marsh’ (Old English fenn).
South German: topographic name from Old High German fenni, Middle Low German and Old Frisian fenne ‘bog’. Compare Fehn .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 馮, see   Feng   1.

variants.name: Fehn
meanings: North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a bog or marsh, from fehn ‘bog’.

Norwegian: habitational name from any of the three farms in southeastern Norway named Fen, probably from Old Norse Fávin, of which the second element is vin ‘meadow’; the first element is unexplained.

variants.name: Fenner
meanings: English: from Middle English fen(ne) ‘fen, marsh’ (Old English fenn) + -er, a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a marsh or fen. Fenn Place in Worth in Sussex had owners called atte Fenne, who in the time of King Henry VI (1421–1471) apparently changed their name to Fenner. See Fenn .
English: variant of Fainer, from Middle English feiner, fener ‘haymonger, one who sells hay’ (Old French fenier), or perhaps a nickname from Middle English feiner ‘inventor; hypocrite’.
South German and Swiss German: occupational name for an ensign or standard bearer, from Middle High German vener, an agent derivative of Middle High German vane ‘flag’. See also Fenrich .

variants.name: Fenning
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English fen(ne) ‘fen, marsh’ (Old English fenn) + -ing, for someone who lived by a fen or marsh. Compare Fenn .

variants.name: Finner
meanings: German: topographic name from an agent derivative of Old High German fenni, Middle Low German and Old Frisian fenne ‘bog’ (see Fenn ).
German: ethnic name for a Finn (see   Finn   4).

en.lifeSummary: De Feni was born in 1060. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: De Feni est né en 1060. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: De Feni wurde 1060 geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: De Feni è nato il nel 1060. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: De Feniは1060年に生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: De Feni(은)는 1060년 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: De Feni nasceu em 1060. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: De Feni родился в 1060 г. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: De Feni nació en 1060. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.

th.1060–: ปี 1060–ถึงแก่กรรม
th.1060–Male: ปี1060–ถึงแก่กรรม
